steps.title: Convert degrees to radians
steps.content: To convert a degree measure to radians, multiply by $\frac{\pi}{180}$. Apply that conversion directly to $120^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Apply the degree-to-radian conversion formula
steps.content:
To convert degrees to radians, multiply the degree measure by $$\frac{\pi}{180}$$.
For $120^{\circ}$:
$$120^\circ \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{120\pi}{180}$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction
steps.content:
Divide both numerator and denominator by 60:
$$\frac{120\pi}{180} = \frac{2\pi}{3} = \frac{2}{3}\pi$$ This is the radian measure equivalent to $120^{\circ}$.
B is correct.
